perturbation.original_text: I/weacknowledge thatwewillreceive confidential information about theSeller's business, operations, customers, andemployees andagree not tostartabusiness similar tothesellers listed above andnottosolicit orcontact Seller's employees orcustomers foraperiod ofthree years from thedateherein. Intheevent Buyer forwhatever reason does notpurchase thebusiness, Buyer agrees toimmediately return toBroker anyandall materials related toSeller without retaining anycopies.
perturbation.changed_text: Intheevent Buyer forwhatever reason does notpurchase thebusiness, Buyer agrees toimmediately return toBroker anyandall materials related toSeller without retaining anycopies. I/weacknowledge thatwewillreceive confidential information about theSeller's business, operations, customers, andemployees andagree not tostartabusiness similar tothesellers listed above andnottosolicit orcontact Seller's employees orcustomers foraperiod ofthree years from thedateherein.
perturbation.explanation: The non-compete and non-solicitation agreement, originally placed within the acknowledgements section, has been moved to the end of the section about returning materials. This placement obscures its importance and enforceability, as it's not clearly highlighted as a critical term of the agreement.
perturbation.law_explanation: Moving the non-compete clause may affect its enforceability under Tennessee law. TCA § 47-25-101 deals with contracts in restraint of trade. While not directly prohibiting non-competes, Tennessee courts scrutinize them for reasonableness, clarity, and whether they protect a legitimate business interest. Obscuring the clause's prominence could be interpreted as evidence that it wasn't fairly presented or understood, potentially weakening its enforceability. The location of the clause contributes to determining whether or not the contract clearly defines the material terms and conditions.
perturbation.justification: The perturbation introduces a structural flaw by relocating the non-compete clause, potentially weakening its enforceability. The Law Explanation points out that Tennessee courts scrutinize non-compete agreements for reasonableness and clarity. While the provided law snippets are missing, the Law Explanation indicates that obscuring the prominence of the clause could be interpreted as evidence that it wasn't fairly presented or understood, potentially weakening its enforceability. The original text clearly places the non-compete agreement within the acknowledgements, whereas the changed text buries it at the end of the section about returning materials, making it less conspicuous. Based on the Perturbation Explanation and Law Explanation, the altered location diminishes the clause's prominence and, therefore, its enforceability.

perturbation.original_text: I/weunderstand thatshould I/webecome amanager orotherwise connect withanyofthebusinesses shown oroffered tome/us forsale, orshould I/webuy,trade, lease orexchange anyofthe businesses disclosed tome/us, thenafeewillbeduetoBroker. I/weunderstand thatifI/wemake thepurchase through Broker, I/wewillnotbe liable forthefeetobepaidbySeller toBroker.
perturbation.changed_text: I/weunderstand thatifI/wemake thepurchase through Broker, I/wewillnotbe liable forthefeetobepaidbySeller toBroker. I/weunderstand thatshould I/webecome amanager orotherwise connect withanyofthebusinesses shown oroffered tome/us forsale, orshould I/webuy,trade, lease orexchange anyofthe businesses disclosed tome/us, thenafeewillbeduetoBroker.
perturbation.explanation: By switching the order of the sentences, the implication is changed. Originally it states the buyer will not pay if purchasing through the broker. However, the edited clause might suggest, that if the buyer is connected with the business, then a fee is due even if they buy through the broker which may create a conflict of interest.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Tennessee Real Estate Broker License Act of 1973, T.C.A. § 62-13-403
perturbation.law_explanation: T.C.A. § 62-13-403 requires brokers to act in the best interest of their client. Placing the sentence regarding fees due if the buyer connects with the business AFTER the sentence stating no fees due if purchase through broker creates an implied conflict of interest and could be seen as misleading, as it could suggest the broker benefits more from the buyer NOT purchasing through the broker (since the seller is responsible for the fees in the purchase through the broker), impacting the broker's obligation to act in the seller's best interest (who is paying the broker).
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: Tennessee Code § 62-13-403 (2021) - Duty Owed to All Parties :: 2021 Tennessee Code :: U.S. Codes and Statutes :: U.S. Law :: Justia There Is a Newer Version of this Section 2024 Tennessee Code 2023 Tennessee Code 2021 Tennessee Code (here) View All Versions View Our Newest Version Here 2021 Tennessee Code Title 62 - Professions, Businesses and Trades Chapter 13 - Tennessee Real Estate Broker License Act of 1973 Part 4 - Representation by Real Estate Agents § 62-13-403. Duty Owed to All Parties Universal Citation: TN Code § 62-13-403 (2021) Learn more This media-neutral citation is based on the American Association of Law Libraries Universal Citation Guide and is not necessarily the official citation. Previous Next A licensee who provides real estate services in a real estate transaction shall owe all parties to the transaction the following duties, except as provided otherwise by § 62-13-405, in addition to other duties specifically set forth in this chapter or the rules of the commission: Diligently exercise reasonable skill and care in providing services to all parties to the transaction; Disclose to each party to the transaction any adverse facts of which the licensee has actual notice or knowledge; Maintain for each party to a transaction the confidentiality of any information obtained by a licensee prior to disclosure to all parties of a written agency or subagency agreement entered into by the licensee to represent either or both of the parties in a transaction. This duty of confidentiality extends to any information that the party would reasonably expect to be held in confidence, except for information that the party has authorized for disclosure, information required to be disclosed under this part and information otherwise required to be disclosed pursuant to this chapter. This duty survives both the subsequent establishment of an agency relationship and the closing of the transaction; Provide services to each party to the transaction with honesty and good faith; Disclose to each p
perturbation.justification: The modified text contradicts T.C.A. § 62-13-403, as explained in the Law Explanation, which requires brokers to act in the best interest of their client. By switching the order of the sentences, the changed text creates a potential conflict of interest where the broker may benefit more if the buyer doesn't purchase through the broker, as the seller pays the fees when the buyer purchases through the broker, potentially compromising the broker's duty to the seller. Scraped Law Snippet 1 supports the broker's duty of good faith and honesty. The Structural Flaws - Legal Contradiction definition states the contradiction arises when a required legal disclosure is placed in a misleading section.
